words.meaning: Not hopeful or encouraging; dreary.
words.example: Prospects of kharif cultivation turn bleak in Telangana with monsoon failure.
words.headline: Prospects of kharif cultivation turns bleak in Telangana with monsoon failure

words.meaning: Anxiety or fear that something bad or unpleasant will happen.
words.example: A democratic society cannot flourish where people continue to live under an environment of constant apprehension.
words.headline: Mehbooba placed under 'house arrest' ahead of Martyrs’ Day; PDP raises rights concerns

words.meaning: Overwhelm with a great flood of something.
words.example: Bangladesh reports 44 deaths due to floods; capital Dhaka deluged.
words.headline: Bangladesh reports 44 deaths due to floods; capital Dhaka deluged

words.meaning: Increase rapidly; make or become more intense or serious.
words.example: U.S. launches more strikes on Iran as standoff over Strait of Hormuz escalates.
words.headline: U.S. launches more strikes on Iran as standoff over Strait of Hormuz escalates

words.meaning: Reduce the quality or value of; treat or regard someone with contempt or disrespect.
words.example: These operations aimed to degrade Iran's capability to attack commercial shipping vessels.
words.headline: Gulf war: US steps up strikes to 'degrade' Iran's ability to attack ships, mariners at Hormuz

words.meaning: To overturn in the water.
words.example: The boat carrying 32 Indian tourists overturned in rough seas off May Rut Ngoai islet.
words.headline: Vietnam arrests speedboat captain after deadly capsize

words.meaning: Reduce (a judicial sentence, especially a sentence of death) to one less severe.
words.example: Suu Kyi is serving a 27-year sentence, recently commuted by one-third.
words.headline: Myanmar tells ASEAN Aung San Suu Kyi will be looked after: envoy

words.meaning: Crying out noisily; characterized by or given to vehement outcry.
words.example: There was public outcry soon after the BJP government renamed Kolkata’s Suhrawardy Avenue.
words.headline: Road rage | Politics of renaming a street in Bengal

words.meaning: Relating to old people, especially with regard to their healthcare.
words.example: Ernakulam to get government-run geriatric centre.
words.headline: Ernakulam to get government-run geriatric centre

words.meaning: The return of someone to their own country.
words.example: The Embassy remains committed to facilitating the earliest repatriation of the bodies.
words.headline: Vietnam boat tragedy: Bodies of 15 Indian tourists to be sent home soon

words.meaning: An outline or a shape, especially one that defines a form.
words.example: Janaki amma’s voice gave contour to the heroines, their fears, their desires, their hopes and their dreams.
words.headline: S. Janaki: The Isai Arasi who established the voice of the rural, female heroine

words.meaning: To reach a climax or point of highest development.
words.example: A years-long U.S. investigation culminated in charges against 37 people linked to alleged transnational crime syndicates.
words.headline: How undercover agents and informants helped the FBI unravel Lawrence Bishnoi’s gang | Explained

words.meaning: A rising and falling in waves; a wavy form or outline.
words.example: The gaping undulations were caused primarily by settlement of the road’s 4-lane carriageway.
words.headline: Committee suggests ₹10-crore project to end undulations on S.A. Road

words.meaning: In a way that is so good or unusual as to be impossible to copy; uniquely.
words.example: Janaki inimitably lent her voice to numerous evergreen Malayalam film songs from the 1960s to the 1990s.
words.headline: S. Janaki’s fresh voice, improvisation made Kerala groove to her tunes

words.meaning: The underlying structure or basic framework of something.
words.example: An artiste whose songs dissolved linguistic boundaries and became woven into the emotional fabric of the State’s cultural life.
words.headline: S. Janaki: Not Kerala-born, but forever its voice

words.meaning: Hostile and aggressive; given to fighting.
words.example: Faced with external security challenges and internal divisions, especially under a sceptical Donald Trump administration, European members are preparing to play a larger role in keeping the alliance united and functional.
words.headline: NATO | Towards a more European alliance

words.meaning: To kill, destroy, or remove a large percentage or part of.
words.example: Earlier Trump had said any attempt to assassinate him would lead the U.S. to ‘completely decimate Iran’.
words.headline: Mojtaba vows revenge for father’s killing after Trump’s new threat

words.meaning: A hostile or argumentative meeting or situation between opposing parties.
words.example: The alliance demonstrated a stronger commitment ‌to bloc-to-bloc confrontation through increased arms spending and closer military ‌cooperation ⁠with allies.
words.headline: North Korea condemns NATO summit; says denuclearisation should begin with U.S. allies

words.meaning: The path followed by a projectile flying or an object moving under the action of given forces; a course of development.
words.example: The legislation is the culmination of a policy trajectory that has been building for over a decade.
words.headline: What’s China’s new ethnic unity law? | Explained

words.meaning: Of, relating to, or characterized by philanthropy; benevolent.
words.example: Melinda French Gates received the Josef Korbel Award for her philanthropic work.
words.headline: Melinda Gates to students: When a woman steps into power, it is better for all

words.meaning: The action of taking something dishonestly for one's own use.
words.example: PM Modi must take responsibility for alleged misappropriation of Ram Temple Trust funds.
words.headline: PM Modi must take responsibility for alleged misappropriation Ram Temple Trust: Rajeev Gowda

words.meaning: Relating to or forming a node; central or connecting.
words.example: KMVN is the nodal agency for the Kailash-Mansarovar Yatra.
words.headline: First batch of Kailash-Mansarovar Yatra pilgrims reaches Tibet

words.meaning: The state of having been abandoned or neglected; willful neglect of duty.
words.example: The introduction of these books was a result of serious negligence and dereliction of duty.
words.headline: How two ‘objectionable’ books triggered a crackdown on J&K’s varsity and college libraries | Explained

words.meaning: Small or meager amount.
words.example: Bihar's power supply increased from a paltry 700 MW in 2005.
words.headline: Bihar’s per capita power consumption increased more than five times to 401 units from 75 in 2005

words.meaning: To murder an important person for political or religious reasons.
words.example: Trump warns 'orders given' if Tehran tries to assassinate him.
words.headline: '1,000 missiles aimed at Iran': Trump warns 'orders given' if Tehran tries to assassinate him

words.meaning: To prevent growth or success; to restrict or stop the flow of something.
words.example: The conflict has throttled worldwide energy supplies.
words.headline: Iran has asked to continue talks, and the U.S. agreed, Trump says

words.meaning: To prove more powerful or superior; to be victorious.
words.example: The truth eventually prevailed in the court case.
words.headline: BJP calls Congress’s charge against Union Home Minister on FCRA ‘baseless and politically motivated’

words.meaning: A period of unrestrained and violent behavior.
words.example: The man went on a murder rampage, killing several people.
words.headline: Murder rampage: Man out on bail kills Pocso victim, her family & then his own; manhunt on

words.meaning: Hostile and aggressive; engaged in war or conflict.
words.example: The warring sides exchanged strikes again this week, displaying their belligerent stance.
words.headline: West Asia war LIVE: Trump threatens Iran after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral saw open calls for his killing

words.meaning: Originating or occurring naturally in a particular place; native.
words.example: INS Mahendragiri is India’s new indigenous stealth frigate.
words.headline: INS Mahendragiri: All you need to know about the Indian Navy’s new indigenous stealth frigate

words.meaning: Marking the beginning of an institution, activity, or series.
words.example: The inaugural TG20, its first franchise-based T20 league, is beginning to change that.
words.headline: Redrawing Telangana’s cricket map

words.meaning: Diminish gradually in size, amount, or strength.
words.example: Neighbourhood shops are finding their fortunes dwindling with the rise of e-commerce platforms.
words.headline: Dwindling Fortunes: Quick-commerce platforms, large retailers threaten small businesses and neighbourhood shops in Kerala

words.meaning: Treat (a person, group, or concept) as insignificant or peripheral.
words.example: The gas leak exposed the socio-economic realities of a marginalised tribal community.
words.headline: Gone girls | Death of 14 Juang girls from Odisha in Tiruvallur ammonia gas leak exposes tribe’s socio-economic struggle

words.meaning: A situation of intense activity, conflict, or competition.
words.example: Jan Suraaj Party founder Prashant Kishor, who is in the fray, has been busy campaigning.
words.headline: Bankipur bypoll: voters say the battle will be between BJP and JSP

words.meaning: Suffer from being forced to remain in an unpleasant or restrictive place or situation.
words.example: The group of nationals languished in Bangladesh for over a year before returning home.
words.headline: Four Indian nationals pushed into Bangladesh return home after one-year legal battle

words.meaning: The state of being negligent or lacking care or attention.
words.example: The bench said slackness by civic authorities against unauthorised constructions will not be tolerated.
words.headline: Supreme Court cites blazes in Delhi, Lucknow; questions civic bodies on unauthorised buildings

words.meaning: The actions of a vigilante, especially taking the law into one's own hands.
words.example: Assault on youths highlights growing vigilantism amid anti-drug crackdown.
words.headline: Assault on youths highlights growing vigilantism in Perumbavoor amid anti-drug crackdown

words.meaning: Put (a person or organization) on a list of those who are disapproved of or are to be punished or excluded.
words.example: The protesters seek legal action against the company, demanding its blacklisting from future projects.
words.headline: Greens demand immediate cancellation of Wayanad tunnel project

words.meaning: Formally accuse of or charge with a serious crime.
words.example: Eight men were indicted on murder and terrorism conspiracy charges.
words.headline: 8 men indicted in planned drone and sniper attack on White House UFC cage-fighting show

words.meaning: Prevent (someone) from accomplishing something.
words.example: The men were indicted for their alleged roles in a thwarted drone and sniper attack.
words.headline: 8 men indicted in planned drone and sniper attack on White House UFC cage-fighting show

words.meaning: Strongly supporting a particular party, cause, or person, often without fair judgement.
words.example: The opposition accused the police of partisan conduct during the protest.
words.headline: 'Police acting as BJP arm': Mamata accuses ruling party of blocking TMC protest

words.meaning: An act of retaliation for a wrong or injury suffered.
words.example: The fresh strikes were launched as a reprisal for attacks on the ships.
words.headline: Trump says fresh strikes on Iran retribution for attacks on ships

words.meaning: Not securely held or in position; dangerously uncertain or unstable.
words.example: The ceasefire in the region remained precarious after fresh attacks.
words.headline: India flags concerns over targeting of commercial ships, escalating Iran-US tensions

words.meaning: Kept secret or done secretively, especially because it is illicit.
words.example: A clandestine operation led to the arrest of the smugglers.
words.headline: How undercover ops led to Operation Hard Ball arrests

words.meaning: Forbidden by law, rules, or custom; illegal.
words.example: Police dismantled a network involved in the illicit drug trade.
words.headline: Chandigarh police bust Pakistan-linked narco terror network after border raids

words.meaning: Likely to cause an argument or disagreement; controversial.
words.example: The donation row soon grew into a contentious issue between the parties.
words.headline: Police question three accused in Ram Temple donation row, SIT to visit Ayodhya

words.meaning: A severe flood or heavy downpour; an overwhelming quantity of something.
words.example: The deluge left homes and markets submerged for days.
words.headline: 13 killed in Surat rains: Floodwaters continue to inundate homes and roads

words.meaning: Hostile and aggressive; engaged in or eager for war.
words.example: The two sides exchanged belligerent threats after the strikes.
words.headline: U.S. launches new strikes on Iran after Trump vows to hit 'hard'

words.meaning: Having developed abilities or maturity at an earlier age than usual.
words.example: The study questioned whether precocious children become successful adults.
words.headline: Scientifically Speaking: Do early winners become winning adults?

words.meaning: So firm or strong as to allow no doubt, change, or exception.
words.example: The allies reaffirmed their ironclad commitment to mutual defence.
words.headline: NATO allies reaffirm 'ironclad commitment' to collective defence

words.meaning: To make a problem or bad situation worse.
words.example: The spread of misinformation is exacerbating real-world harm to refugees.
words.headline: AI-powered misinformation inciting harm to refugees, says UN

words.meaning: A future event that is possible but cannot be predicted with certainty; a plan made for such an event.
words.example: The district was asked to draw up contingency plans for a possible drought.
words.headline: Prepare for possible drought, drinking water crisis, Minister tells officials

words.meaning: To become dry and shrivelled; to fade or decline.
words.example: The red gram saplings began to wither as the rains failed.
words.headline: Red gram farmers watch saplings wilt, hope wither as Karnataka awaits rains

words.meaning: To formally accuse or charge someone with a serious crime.
words.example: US authorities indicted 37 gangsters in connection with the case.
words.headline: Op Hard Ball: 1,000kg cocaine, murder and extortion charges, Bishnoi among 37 gangsters indicted by US

words.meaning: The act of obtaining something, especially money, through force or threats.
words.example: The gang faced charges of murder and extortion.
words.headline: Op Hard Ball: 1,000kg cocaine, murder and extortion charges, Bishnoi among 37 gangsters indicted by US

words.meaning: The murder of a prominent person, often for political or ideological reasons.
words.example: The accused were charged with ordering the assassination of a community leader.
words.headline: FBI $50,000 reward for Goldy Brar, fresh raids: Why Lawrence Bishnoi gang is back in focus

words.meaning: Made impure or lower in quality by adding an inferior or harmful substance.
words.example: He called paneer the most adulterated food on the market.
words.headline: Former BCCI chef calls paneer most adulterated food

words.meaning: A group of people or organisations combined to promote a common, often illicit, interest.
words.example: The police cracked down on the smuggling syndicate.
words.headline: 'Corrupt Punjab cop' at heart of US crackdown on Jaggu Bhagwanpuria syndicate

words.meaning: To prevent something bad from happening; to turn away.
words.example: A potential runway collision was averted at the airport.
words.headline: Close call: Air India, AI Express planes come face-to-face at Mumbai airport

words.meaning: The forming of a theory or opinion without firm evidence; conjecture.
words.example: The meeting sparked speculation about a new political alliance.
words.headline: Another split brewing? Sharad NCP's Jayant Patil meets BJP leader Vinod Tawde
